title: cookie-session with secure: false — the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP and can be sniffed

params.passDetail: cookie-session sets secure: true — the signed session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.
params.failDetail: cookie-session is configured with secure: false, so the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can read the signed session and hijack the user's authenticated session. Set secure: true in production (behind `app.set('trust proxy', 1)` if terminating TLS at a proxy).
params.absentCallDetail: No cookieSession() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: cookieSession() does not set secure; verify the deployment forces HTTPS-only cookies (secure: true) in production.

provenance.note: cookie-session takes cookie flags (secure/httpOnly/sameSite) at the top level of its options. secure: false is a common copy-paste from local-dev config that ships to production and exposes the session over HTTP.
